title: cleome psoraleifolia var. trachycarpa
intro: Cleome psoraleifolia var. trachycarpa is a plant species within the Cleome genus, commonly known as the spider flower, characterized by its unique, delicate appearance and medicinal properties. This plant is known for its seeds, which contain furanocoumarins, a compound with phototoxic properties that can be used to treat various health conditions. The seeds of Cleome psoraleifolia var. trachycarpa have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat skin conditions, such as vitiligo and psoriasis, due to their ability to stimulate melanin production and improve skin texture. In herbal preparations, the seeds are typically dried and ground into a powder or infused in oil to create topical creams or ointments for treating skin issues.
